data.title: Rolls-Royce GAAP EPS of 52.15p, revenue of £9.49B, raises FY25 guidance
data.description:
• Rolls-Royce press release (OTCPK:RYCEY): 1H GAAP EPS of 52.15p.
  • Underlying operating profit rose by 50% to £1.7bn with a margin of 19.1%, reflecting the impact of our strategic initiatives, operational effectiveness, and performance management
  • Revenue of £9.49B (+7.1% Y/Y).
  • Full year 2025 guidance raised: Expects £3.1bn-£3.2bn underlying operating profit and £3.0bn- £3.1bn free cash flow

data.title: Air Products tops estimates despite lower volumes; raises outlook for full-year EPS
data.description:
Air Products and Chemicals (NYSE:APD) reported fiscal third-quarter earnings that beat Wall Street expectations Thursday, as higher pricing, favorable currency and cost productivity offset lower helium demand and the effect of project exits.

The industrial gases company posted adjusted earnings of $3.09 a share, topping analysts’ forecast of $2.99. Revenue came in at $3.02 billion, edging past the consensus estimate of $2.99 billion and up 1% year-over-year. GAAP earnings were $3.24 a share, up 4% from the same quarter last year.

While the company faced a 4% drop in volumes -- linked to a previously announced LNG business sale, softer global helium demand, and exited projects -- pricing gains and cost reductions helped cushion results. Adjusted operating income was $741 million, roughly flat compared to last year.

“The Air Products team delivered solid results this quarter that exceeded guidance and were higher than last year on a comparable basis, excluding the impact of the LNG sale,” Chief Executive Eduardo Menezes said in a statement. “We are staying focused on our cost productivity efforts, pricing, operational excellence and capital discipline.”

Regionally, Asia and Europe showed volume growth and improved margins, while the Americas segment was pressured by lower helium sales and maintenance-related costs. Income from Middle East and India affiliates declined 4%.

Looking ahead, Air Products (NYSE:APD) tightened its full-year adjusted EPS guidance to $11.90 to $12.10, and forecast fourth-quarter adjusted EPS of $3.27 to $3.47. Capital expenditures for fiscal 2025 are expected to reach about $5 billion, as the company continues investing in clean hydrogen and industrial gas infrastructure.

data.title: HeidelbergCement reports 1H results
data.description:
• HeidelbergCement press release (OTCPK:HDLMY): 1H Group revenue rose by 4.0% in comparison with the previous year to €10,398 million (previous year: €9,994M).
  • EPS of €3.89

data.title: Fox buys stake in IndyCar owner Penske Entertainment - WSJ
data.description:
Fox Corp. (NASDAQ:FOXA) purchased a one-third stake in Penske Entertainment, owner of auto racing's IndyCar Series and Indianapolis Motor Speedway.

Terms of the detail weren't given, though Fox's (NASDAQ:FOXA) investment is said to be between $125 million and $135 million, according to a WSJ report  on Thursday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

Under the agreement, Fox's (FOXA) media-rights for IndyCar races have been extended for several years, the companies said. Fox began carrying IndyCar earlier this year.

data.title: Builders FirstSource stock sinks after Q2 earnings miss on weaker housing demand
data.description:
Shares of Builders FirstSource (NYSE:BLDR) tumbled 7.9% in premarket trading Thursday after the building materials supplier posted second-quarter results that came in below Wall Street expectations, hurt by a soft housing market and margin pressures.

The company reported earnings of $1.66 a share, missing the consensus estimate of $1.92, and down from $2.87 a year earlier. Adjusted earnings of $2.38 a share were ahead of the consensus estimate of $2.34.

Net sales fell 5% to $4.23 billion, narrowly missing consensus estimate of $4.28 billion, as declines in single- and multi-family construction more than offset modest gains in repair and remodel activity.

Gross profit margins narrowed by 210 basis points to 30.7%, reflecting what management described as “margin normalization” in core housing markets and a below-normal pace of new home starts. Adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization dropped 24% to $506 million, with margins slipping to 12%.

Net income slid to $185 million, a 46% decline from the prior year, as lower sales volumes and rising interest expenses outweighed cost-saving initiatives. Free cash flow fell nearly 31% to $255 million, while cash from operations totaled $341 million for the quarter.

Chief Executive Peter Jackson acknowledged the challenging conditions but emphasized long-term priorities.

“We are prioritizing what’s within our control -- serving customers with excellence, leveraging technology, and managing the business with discipline,” Jackson said in a statement. “These efforts are strengthening our position in the industry.”

The results underscore ongoing weakness in U.S. homebuilding activity, particularly in multi-family projects, which saw sales decline more than 23% from a year ago. The company also cited commodity price deflation and reduced operating leverage as additional headwinds.

Builders FirstSource (NYSE:BLDR) repurchased 3.3 million shares in the quarter, bringing its cumulative buybacks to nearly half of its total outstanding shares since 2021. The company reaffirmed its full-year outlook but warned that single-family housing starts could be down as much as 12% in 2025 compared with last year.

data.title: Alps Alpine reports FQ1 results
data.description:
• Alps Alpine press release (OTCPK:APELY): FQ1 Profit Attributable to Owners of Parent of -¥2.8B

data.title: Allegiant sees June passenger traffic increase 9.2%
data.description:
• Allegiant Travel Company (NASDAQ:ALGT) on Thursday reported a 9.6% increase in scheduled service in June nearly to 2M, driving up the total number of miles flown by paying passengers (revenue passenger miles) by nearly 9.8% to 1.77M.
  • Available seat miles rose more than 12.4% to 2.09M.
  • ALGT +1.99% premarket to $51.68.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: HENSOLDT AG ADR reports 1H results

  • Revenue grows to EUR 944 million in the first half of 2025 (previous year: EUR 849 million)

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Mastercard tops Q2 estimates; initiates Q3 and updates FY outlook
Dyadic International prices public offering of 6.05M shares of common stock
data.description:
• Dyadic International (NASDAQ:DYAI) announced on Thursday the pricing of its previously announced underwritten public offering of 6.05M shares of common stock at a public offering price of $0.95 per share. 
  • The company expects the gross proceeds from this offering to be approximately $5.75M, before deducting the underwriting discount and other offering expenses. 
  • Dyadic intends to use the net proceeds from the offering for working capital and general corporate purposes, such as product development, sales, and marketing.
